task_metadata.current_task: Implement put(key, value) with eviction logic - evict LRU item when at capacity
task_metadata.status: Designing individual operations - put method with eviction

nodes.description: Choose data structure for LRU cache implementation - use HashMap + Doubly Linked List
nodes.rationale: HashMap provides O(1) get operations, while Doubly Linked List maintains insertion/access order for O(1) eviction. This combination achieves optimal time complexity for both get and put operations
nodes.task_state: Starting LRU cache implementation - defining core architecture

nodes.description: LRU cache must respect a fixed capacity limit
nodes.rationale: LRU (Least Recently Used) eviction is only meaningful with a capacity constraint. When cache is full, the least recently used item must be evicted before inserting new items

nodes.description: Implement get(key) to return value and move accessed node to front of linked list
nodes.rationale: Get operation must mark the accessed item as most recently used by moving it to the front of the linked list. This ensures that least recently used items remain at the back for eviction

nodes.description: Implement put(key, value) with eviction logic - evict LRU item when at capacity
nodes.rationale: Put operation must handle three cases: (1) key exists - update value and move to front, (2) key doesn't exist and under capacity - add to front, (3) key doesn't exist and at capacity - evict from back, then add to front
nodes.task_state: Designing individual operations - put method with eviction

edges.rationale: The HashMap + Doubly Linked List architecture design must account for the capacity constraint which drives the eviction logic
Get and put methods are logically sequenced - get establishes how items are marked as recently used, which put then leverages for eviction decisions
